name: Current Affairs in History & National Movement (9): Lothal's First Major Excavation in 65 Years

sourceRefs.label: lothal.org - Lothal excavation resumed March 2025 (first major dig since 1955-1962); gold particles, unicorn seal, and other 2025-26 discoveries

researchNotes: Tenth History current-affairs topic (9th lap). Lothal — a core Harappan/Indus Valley site already well-known from the standard syllabus — gets a genuine current-affairs update via this renewed excavation, reinforcing core content rather than sitting apart from it. Expansion pass (Batch 22): added Lothal's state/river location for basic geography grounding.

reading.intro: Covers the renewed excavation at Lothal — the ancient Harappan port city famous for the world's oldest known dockyard — its first major dig in 65 years.

qa.question: When did active excavation resume at Lothal, and why is this notable?
qa.answer: Active excavation began in March 2025 (orders issued January 2025) — the first MAJOR dig at the site in 65 years; the previous large-scale excavation was conducted between 1955-1962.

qa.question: What factors drove this renewed excavation effort?
qa.answer: Modern technological advances enabling more precise analysis; previously unexplored areas from the original dig; the development of the National Maritime Heritage Complex project at the site; and a goal of creating 'live excavation alongside museum exhibitions' for visitors.

qa.question: What are some key discoveries from this 2025-26 excavation?
qa.answer: Tiny gold particles (suggesting metalworking/trade activity); 3 seals, including one depicting a UNICORN; bone fragments and fish remains (otoliths); drill bits used for bead-making; brick structures including ramp flooring; and terracotta artifacts.

qa.question: What modern methods are being used in this excavation?
qa.answer: Drone surveys, digital mapping, and carbon dating of approximately 30 samples — the site has been divided into 3 research zones (A, B, C), with over 50 local workers employed.

qa.question: What is Lothal famous for, in standard Indus Valley Civilization history?
qa.answer: It is home to the world's oldest known dockyard — evidence of the Harappan civilization's advanced maritime trade infrastructure.

qa.question: In which present-day Indian state is Lothal located, and near which river/gulf?
qa.answer: Gujarat, near the Gulf of Khambhat (Cambay), on the Bhogavo river — a location consistent with its role as a coastal/riverine trading port in the Harappan network.
